Title: Verify layout reflow at the 768px tablet breakpoint boundary
Preconditions: RefApp home page reachable; browser devtools available
Test_Steps: 1. Open the home page at viewport width 769px | 2. Note multi-column app tile layout | 3. Resize viewport to exactly 768px | 4. Resize to 767px | 5. Compare layouts at each step
Expected_Result: At >=768px the desktop/tablet grid renders; at <768px the layout collapses to the mobile single/stacked column without overlap or clipping at the boundary

Title: Validate app tile grid columns across standard device widths
Preconditions: RefApp home page reachable
Test_Steps: 1. Load home page at 320px | 2. Reload at 375px | 3. Reload at 414px | 4. Reload at 768px | 5. Reload at 1024px | 6. Record column count and tile wrapping at each width
Expected_Result: Tile columns increase monotonically with width; tiles never overflow the viewport; no half-clipped tile at any tested width

Title: Verify extreme narrow viewport (280px) does not break core navigation
Preconditions: Logged in user on home page
Test_Steps: 1. Set viewport to 280px (Galaxy Fold cover) | 2. Load home page | 3. Attempt to open the main menu | 4. Tap a primary app tile
Expected_Result: Content remains readable and tappable; menu opens; navigation succeeds without horizontal scroll or content cutoff

Title: Confirm no horizontal scrollbar on patient dashboard at 360px
Preconditions: Patient with active visit exists; clinician logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard at 360px width | 2. Scroll vertically end to end | 3. Attempt horizontal swipe/drag | 4. Inspect document.scrollWidth vs window.innerWidth
Expected_Result: No horizontal scroll occurs; scrollWidth <= innerWidth; all dashboard widgets fit within the viewport

Title: Detect horizontal overflow caused by long unbroken patient identifier
Preconditions: Patient with a long identifier/name exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient header at 360px for a patient with a 40-char identifier | 2. Observe header rendering | 3. Check for horizontal scroll
Expected_Result: Long identifier wraps or truncates with ellipsis; no horizontal scroll is introduced by the long token

Title: Wide data table degrades gracefully on mobile
Preconditions: Patient with many encounters/obs exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open an encounter list/obs table at 375px | 2. Observe table rendering | 3. Attempt to view all columns
Expected_Result: Table either becomes horizontally scrollable within its own container (not the page) or stacks into cards; the page body itself has no horizontal scroll

Title: Verify navbar collapses into hamburger menu below breakpoint
Preconditions: Logged in user; RefApp navbar present
Test_Steps: 1. Load any page at 1200px and confirm expanded nav links | 2. Resize to 600px | 3. Observe the navbar | 4. Confirm a hamburger/toggle control appears
Expected_Result: Above breakpoint nav links are inline; below breakpoint links collapse into a hamburger toggle control that is visible and tappable

Title: Toggle collapsible navbar open and closed (state transition)
Preconditions: Page loaded at 480px with hamburger visible
Test_Steps: 1. Observe collapsed state (menu hidden) | 2. Tap hamburger -> menu expands | 3. Tap hamburger again -> menu collapses | 4. Tap a menu item -> navigation occurs and menu collapses
Expected_Result: States transition Collapsed->Expanded->Collapsed correctly; selecting an item navigates and auto-collapses the menu

Title: Navbar menu closes when tapping outside the menu
Preconditions: Hamburger menu expanded at mobile width
Test_Steps: 1. Open the hamburger menu | 2. Tap on the page area outside the menu | 3. Observe menu state
Expected_Result: Tapping outside collapses the open menu; no orphaned/half-open menu state remains

Title: Keyboard accessibility of collapsed navbar toggle
Preconditions: Mobile width; external keyboard or tab navigation available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab to the hamburger toggle | 2. Activate with Enter and Space | 3. Tab into the expanded menu items | 4. Activate a menu item with Enter
Expected_Result: Toggle is focusable and operable via Enter/Space; aria-expanded reflects open/closed; menu items are reachable and operable by keyboard

Title: Screen reader announces navbar toggle state
Preconditions: Mobile width; screen reader (VoiceOver/TalkBack) active
Test_Steps: 1. Focus the hamburger toggle with screen reader | 2. Listen to the announced role and state | 3. Expand the menu | 4. Listen to updated state announcement
Expected_Result: Toggle is announced as a button with expandable state; aria-expanded toggles true/false and is announced on change

Title: Verify primary action buttons meet 44x44 CSS px minimum touch target
Preconditions: Mobile width; patient dashboard with action buttons
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard at 375px | 2. Measure computed hit area of Save/Start Visit/primary buttons | 3. Compare against 44x44 CSS px
Expected_Result: All primary interactive controls have a touch target of at least 44x44 CSS px (WCAG 2.5.5 / Apple HIG)

Title: Boundary check of adjacent touch targets spacing
Preconditions: Mobile width; list with closely packed row actions
Test_Steps: 1. Open a list with edit/delete row icons at 360px | 2. Measure spacing between adjacent targets | 3. Tap each repeatedly to check mis-taps
Expected_Result: Adjacent targets have adequate spacing (>=8px) so repeated taps hit the intended control without accidental activation of neighbors

Title: Negative: tiny icon-only control fails minimum target size
Preconditions: Mobile width; page containing small icon controls
Test_Steps: 1. Identify icon-only controls (close X, info) at 375px | 2. Measure hit area | 3. Flag any below 44x44 CSS px
Expected_Result: Any control below 44x44 CSS px is reported as a defect; test fails if such controls exist on primary clinical flows

Title: Layout adapts correctly on portrait to landscape rotation
Preconditions: Mobile device/emulator; patient dashboard open
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard in portrait | 2. Rotate device to landscape | 3. Observe reflow | 4. Rotate back to portrait
Expected_Result: Layout reflows on rotation without content loss, overlap, or horizontal scroll; scroll position and entered form data are preserved

Title: Form in-progress data persists across orientation change
Preconditions: Clinician entering a clinical form on mobile
Test_Steps: 1. Begin entering a vitals/clinical form in portrait | 2. Fill several fields | 3. Rotate to landscape mid-entry | 4. Verify entered values remain
Expected_Result: All entered field values persist after rotation; no field is reset or lost; focus does not jump unexpectedly

Title: Landscape on small-height device keeps action bar reachable
Preconditions: Small device in landscape (e.g., 667x375)
Test_Steps: 1. Open a long clinical form in landscape | 2. Scroll to bottom | 3. Locate Save/Cancel actions
Expected_Result: Action bar is reachable (sticky or scrollable into view); the on-screen keyboard does not permanently hide the submit action

Title: Verify viewport meta tag is present and correctly configured
Preconditions: Access to page HTML source
Test_Steps: 1. Load the app on mobile | 2. View page source | 3. Inspect the meta name=viewport tag content
Expected_Result: A viewport meta tag exists with width=device-width and initial-scale=1 so the page renders at device width rather than a zoomed-out desktop layout

Title: Negative: pinch-zoom must not be disabled (no user-scalable=no)
Preconditions: Access to page HTML source; mobile device
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect viewport meta tag | 2. Check for user-scalable=no or maximum-scale=1 | 3. On device, attempt pinch-zoom on text
Expected_Result: Viewport meta does not disable user scaling; users can pinch-zoom content to at least 200% (WCAG 1.4.4); test fails if user-scalable=no is present

Title: Login form usable and fully visible at 320px
Preconditions: Logout state; mobile width 320px
Test_Steps: 1. Open login page at 320px | 2. Confirm username, password, login fields visible | 3. Enter credentials | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: All login fields and the submit button are visible and usable without horizontal scroll; login succeeds

Title: Correct mobile keyboard type for numeric vitals fields
Preconditions: Clinician on a vitals form on a real mobile device
Test_Steps: 1. Open vitals form on mobile | 2. Focus a numeric field (pulse) | 3. Observe the on-screen keyboard | 4. Focus a date/time field
Expected_Result: Numeric fields invoke a numeric keypad (inputmode/type appropriate); date fields invoke a date picker; reduces entry errors on mobile

Title: On-screen keyboard does not obscure the focused input
Preconditions: Mobile device; long clinical form
Test_Steps: 1. Focus an input near the bottom of a long form | 2. Observe scroll behavior when keyboard appears | 3. Type a value
Expected_Result: The focused input scrolls into view above the on-screen keyboard and remains visible while typing

Title: Negative: required-field validation visible without horizontal scroll on mobile
Preconditions: Mobile width; registration/clinical form with required fields
Test_Steps: 1. Open form at 360px | 2. Submit with required fields empty | 3. Observe validation messages
Expected_Result: Inline validation messages render fully within the viewport, are associated with their fields, and do not cause horizontal scroll or overlap

Title: Patient registration multi-section form navigable on mobile
Preconditions: Registration privilege; mobile width
Test_Steps: 1. Start patient registration at 375px | 2. Complete demographics section | 3. Advance to contact/address section | 4. Complete and submit
Test_Data: Width=375 px; Name=Syn Test; Gender=F; DOB=1990-01-01
Expected_Result: Each form section is navigable, fields are reachable, section navigation works, and the patient is created successfully

Title: Date of birth / date picker usable on touch screen
Preconditions: Mobile device; form with a date field
Test_Steps: 1. Open a form with a DOB field on mobile | 2. Tap the date field | 3. Select a date via the picker | 4. Confirm value
Expected_Result: Date picker opens on tap, supports touch selection, and the selected date is recorded in the expected format

Title: Breadcrumb / back navigation works on mobile flows
Preconditions: Logged in; multi-level navigation available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate Home -> Find Patient -> Patient Dashboard on mobile | 2. Use back/breadcrumb to return | 3. Verify each level
Expected_Result: Back/breadcrumb navigation returns to the correct prior context without losing state or producing a broken/blank page

Title: Sticky header/action bar remains accessible while scrolling
Preconditions: Mobile width; long-content page
Test_Steps: 1. Open a long page at 375px | 2. Scroll down significantly | 3. Observe header/action bar | 4. Trigger a header action
Expected_Result: Sticky elements (header or primary actions) remain reachable while scrolling; they do not overlap or hide content needed for the action

Title: Active visit context banner visible and not truncated on mobile
Preconditions: Patient with active visit; clinician logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with active visit at 360px | 2. Observe the active visit banner/context | 3. Verify key context (visit type, location, start)
Expected_Result: Active visit context is visible and key details are legible (wrapped/truncated gracefully); clinician can identify the visit before charting

Title: Home page first contentful paint within target on throttled mobile
Preconditions: Network throttling (Fast 3G) and CPU 4x slowdown configured
Test_Steps: 1. Clear cache | 2. Throttle to Fast 3G + 4x CPU | 3. Load home page | 4. Record FCP and TTI from devtools/Lighthouse
Expected_Result: FCP is within the agreed mobile budget (e.g., <3s) and the page becomes interactive without long main-thread blocking; record actual values

Title: Mobile bundle/asset transfer size within budget
Preconditions: Devtools network tab available
Test_Steps: 1. Load home page on a cold cache mobile profile | 2. Record total transfer size and number of requests | 3. Identify largest assets
Expected_Result: Total initial transfer size is within the defined performance budget; no single oversized uncompressed asset dominates load

Title: Images served responsively / not oversized for mobile
Preconditions: Devtools available; pages with images/logos
Test_Steps: 1. Load pages with imagery at 375px | 2. Inspect rendered vs intrinsic image dimensions | 3. Check srcset/sizes usage
Expected_Result: Images are not served at dimensions far larger than rendered; responsive image techniques (srcset) or appropriately sized assets are used

Title: Layout stability (no excessive content shift) on mobile load
Preconditions: Lighthouse/devtools CLS measurement available
Test_Steps: 1. Load patient dashboard on mobile profile | 2. Observe content as it loads | 3. Measure Cumulative Layout Shift
Expected_Result: CLS is within acceptable threshold (<0.1); content does not jump such that a tap lands on the wrong control

Title: Text reflows at 320px without loss of content (WCAG 1.4.10)
Preconditions: Mobile width; content-heavy clinical page
Test_Steps: 1. Open a content page at 320px CSS width (or 400% zoom on 1280px) | 2. Read all content | 3. Check for two-dimensional scrolling
Expected_Result: All content and functionality remain available with only vertical scrolling; no information or function is lost (WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow)

Title: Text spacing override does not clip content on mobile
Preconditions: Mobile width; ability to inject text-spacing CSS
Test_Steps: 1. Apply WCAG 1.4.12 text-spacing overrides at 375px | 2. Review forms and lists | 3. Check for clipped/overlapping text
Expected_Result: With increased line/letter/word/paragraph spacing, no content is clipped or overlapped and all functions remain usable

Title: Focus order remains logical in collapsed mobile layout
Preconditions: Mobile width; keyboard available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through the page at 480px | 2. Track focus order through nav, content, actions | 3. Confirm focus visible
Expected_Result: Focus order follows a logical reading sequence in the reflowed layout; focus indicator is visible on every interactive element

Title: Color contrast holds for mobile-specific UI elements
Preconditions: Mobile width; contrast analyzer
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect hamburger icon, mobile menu items, badges at 375px | 2. Measure contrast ratios | 3. Compare against 4.5:1 (text) / 3:1 (UI)
Expected_Result: Text meets >=4.5:1 and UI/icon components meet >=3:1 contrast in mobile presentation (WCAG 1.4.3 / 1.4.11)

Title: Decision table: form submit state by network and validation on mobile
Preconditions: Mobile device; clinical form
Test_Steps: 1. Combine conditions: ValidInput {Y,N} x Online {Y,N} | 2. Attempt submit for each combination | 3. Record outcome
Expected_Result: (Y,Y)=saved; (Y,N)=blocked or queued with clear offline message; (N,Y)=validation error shown; (N,N)=validation error takes precedence, no data loss

Title: Pairwise: navigation entry across device x orientation x auth-role
Preconditions: Test accounts for clerk and clinician roles
Test_Steps: 1. Combine Device{phone,tablet} x Orientation{portrait,landscape} x Role{clerk,clinician} | 2. Log in and open primary landing per combo | 3. Verify role-appropriate nav items
Expected_Result: For each combination the correct role-scoped navigation renders responsively with no broken layout or missing privileged items

Title: Exploratory: rapid resize stress of patient dashboard
Preconditions: Patient dashboard open; resizable browser
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly resize window between 320px and 1280px repeatedly | 2. Observe for flicker, stuck states, or overlap | 3. Interact mid-resize
Expected_Result: Layout recovers cleanly to the correct breakpoint after rapid resizing; no stuck overlays, orphaned menus, or unclickable regions remain

Title: Exploratory: interrupt mobile form entry with incoming call / app switch
Preconditions: Real mobile device; clinical form in progress
Test_Steps: 1. Begin entering a clinical form | 2. Switch to another app / simulate call | 3. Return to the browser after delay | 4. Inspect form state
Expected_Result: On returning, the form session and entered data are preserved (or the user is clearly warned of session expiry); no silent data loss

Title: Session timeout warning is visible and actionable on mobile
Preconditions: Configured idle session timeout; mobile width
Test_Steps: 1. Log in on mobile and remain idle near timeout | 2. Observe the timeout/warning UI at 375px | 3. Attempt to extend session
Expected_Result: Timeout warning renders fully within the mobile viewport and its extend/continue action is tappable; session security is enforced

Title: Sensitive fields are masked and not autofilled insecurely on mobile
Preconditions: Login/credential form on mobile
Test_Steps: 1. Open login on mobile | 2. Confirm password field masks input | 3. Check autocomplete handling for credentials | 4. Verify no PHI cached in URL
Expected_Result: Password input is masked; credential autocomplete behaves per policy; no PHI/credentials are exposed in query strings or visible plaintext

Title: Negative: deep link to patient on mobile enforces authentication
Preconditions: Valid patient dashboard URL captured; unauthenticated mobile session
Test_Steps: 1. Open a direct patient dashboard URL on mobile while logged out | 2. Observe behavior
Expected_Result: Unauthenticated access is blocked and redirected to login; no PHI is rendered before authentication regardless of mobile layout

Title: REST API returns same patient data regardless of mobile client
Preconditions: Valid API session token
Test_Steps: 1. Call GET patient resource via REST from a mobile user-agent | 2. Call the same endpoint from desktop UA | 3. Compare payloads
Expected_Result: API response content is identical across user agents; responsiveness is purely client-side; no server-side data divergence by device

Title: FHIR Patient read consumed by mobile client renders correctly
Preconditions: FHIR module enabled; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET Patient/{id} from the FHIR R4 endpoint | 2. Render key fields (name, gender, birthDate, identifier) in the mobile view | 3. Verify mapping
Expected_Result: FHIR Patient resource fields map correctly to the mobile UI; name/gender/birthDate/identifier display accurately and fit the mobile layout

Title: FHIR Bundle pagination usable within mobile list view
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint with paginated results
Test_Steps: 1. Request a searchset Bundle (e.g., Observation) with _count | 2. Render first page in a mobile list | 3. Follow next link | 4. Verify continuity
Expected_Result: Each Bundle page renders within the mobile viewport without horizontal scroll; next/prev links work and no records are duplicated or skipped

Title: HL7 ADT-driven patient appears correctly in mobile patient search
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface configured (Platform); ADT^A04 feed
Test_Steps: 1. Send a synthetic ADT^A04 registration message | 2. Wait for ingestion | 3. Search the patient in the mobile UI | 4. Verify demographics
Expected_Result: The HL7-registered patient is findable in mobile search and demographics match the message; mobile presentation is correct and complete

Title: Audit log records mobile-originated PHI access
Preconditions: Audit/logging enabled; DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Access a patient record from a mobile session | 2. Query the audit/log store for the access event | 3. Verify user, patient, timestamp, and access type
Expected_Result: An audit record is persisted for the mobile PHI access with correct actor, target, action, and timestamp; mobile access is not exempt from auditing

Title: Decision table: layout mode by viewport width band
Preconditions: Home page reachable
Test_Steps: 1. For width bands <576, 576-767, 768-991, >=992 load the page | 2. Record layout mode | 3. Verify expected columns/nav per band
Expected_Result: Each width band maps to its expected layout mode (mobile stacked / tablet / desktop) consistently per the design grid with no unexpected jumps

Title: Swipe/scroll gesture does not accidentally trigger destructive action
Preconditions: Mobile device; list with swipeable/row actions
Test_Steps: 1. On a list at 375px perform vertical scroll over rows with delete actions | 2. Perform horizontal swipe near a delete icon | 3. Observe outcomes
Expected_Result: Normal scrolling never triggers a destructive action; any swipe-to-delete requires confirmation; no accidental data deletion occurs

Title: Long picklist / concept search usable on small screen
Preconditions: Clinical form with a searchable concept/coded field on mobile
Test_Steps: 1. Open a coded field (e.g., diagnosis) at 375px | 2. Type to search | 3. Scroll the result list | 4. Select an item
Expected_Result: The search dropdown fits the viewport, is scrollable, results are tappable, and the selected concept is captured correctly

Title: Pairwise: app tile launch across browser x device width
Preconditions: Multiple mobile browsers available
Test_Steps: 1. Combine Browser{Chrome,Safari} x Width{360,768} | 2. Tap a primary app tile per combo | 3. Verify destination loads responsively
Expected_Result: For each combination the tile launches its app and the destination renders responsively without layout breakage or horizontal scroll

Title: State transition: offline to online while using mobile web app
Preconditions: Mobile device; app loaded; network toggle available
Test_Steps: 1. Use the app online | 2. Disable network | 3. Attempt an action | 4. Re-enable network | 5. Retry the action
Expected_Result: Offline state shows a clear non-blocking message; on reconnect, normal operation resumes and no duplicate/lost submissions occur

Title: Touch target and reflow validated on RTL locale at mobile width
Preconditions: RTL locale configured (if supported); mobile width
Test_Steps: 1. Switch UI to an RTL locale | 2. Load home and a form at 375px | 3. Verify mirrored layout, reflow, and touch targets
Expected_Result: RTL layout mirrors correctly, reflows without horizontal scroll, and touch targets/menus remain usable; no clipped or misaligned controls

Title: Negative: screenshot/clipboard of PHI handled per policy on mobile
Preconditions: Mobile device; PHI displayed; org policy defined
Test_Steps: 1. Display a patient record on mobile | 2. Attempt to copy PHI to clipboard | 3. Observe any policy controls or warnings
Expected_Result: Clipboard/screenshot handling aligns with the documented policy (warned or restricted where required); no unexpected PHI leakage path is enabled by the mobile UI
